name: Catuai

migrationPath.notes: Bred from Caturra x Mundo Novo cross
Widely adopted for high yield and wind resistance

description: A cross between Caturra and Mundo Novo bred at the Instituto Agronômico de Campinas in Brazil. It combines Caturra's compact size with Mundo Novo's vigor and productivity. Catuai became one of the most widely planted varieties in Latin America during the 1970s–80s, valued for high yields and wind resistance. It comes in yellow (Amarillo) and red (Rojo) fruiting variants. While productive, it lacks the cup distinctiveness of heirloom varieties.
